intro: Uragoga chlorantha, also known as Uragoga, is a plant species native to tropical regions, particularly in the West Indies and Central America. Its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga chlorantha include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Uragoga is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used to make teas and infusions for the treatment of various health conditions.
